data.title: South Korea Reviews Crypto Custody Provider After Security Breach
data.description: South Korea's National Tax Service is evaluating a private custody provider for seized cryptocurrencies following a security breach earlier this year. According to NS3.AI, the incident involved a leaked seed phrase in February, resulting in an unauthorized transfer of approximately $4.8 million in tokens. The agency is currently drafting criteria for selecting a provider and plans to finalize the choice by the first half of 2026. This review comes in the wake of another custody failure involving 22 BTC and a cross-agency investigation into the management of seized digital assets.

data.title: Perpetual DEX Trading Volumes Show Mixed Trends
data.description:
Recent data from DefiLlama indicates varied trends in trading volumes across several perpetual decentralized exchanges (Perp DEX). According to BlockBeats, while some platforms have experienced a decline in trading activity and a slight contraction in open interest, others have shown growth.

Hyperliquid has seen a rise in trading volume compared to the previous day, maintaining a high level of open interest. EdgeX, possibly driven by anticipation of its launch, remains active and holds the second position in trading volume.

The latest rankings for mainstream Perp DEX trading volumes are as follows:

Hyperliquid recorded a 24-hour trading volume of approximately $9.37 billion, with open contracts valued at around $7.0 billion.

EdgeX reported a 24-hour trading volume of about $3.54 billion, with open contracts totaling approximately $1.11 billion.

Aster's 24-hour trading volume was approximately $3.09 billion, with open contracts valued at around $1.86 billion.

Lighter had a 24-hour trading volume of about $1.85 billion, with open contracts totaling approximately $722 million.

Grvt recorded a 24-hour trading volume of approximately $1.54 billion, with open contracts valued at around $490 million.

Pacifica's 24-hour trading volume was about $441 million, with open contracts totaling approximately $75.81 million.

data.title: Middle East Conflict Spurs Oil Price Surge, Gas Supply Remains Stable
data.description: The recent outbreak of conflict in the Middle East has led to a sharp increase in oil prices. According to Ming Pao, Wong Wai-yee, Managing Director of Towngas (0003), stated that natural gas constitutes 60% of Towngas's supply. Due to a long-term 'take or pay' contract initiated in 2006 with Australian natural gas suppliers, Towngas is insulated from market price fluctuations. Since the implementation of this contract, the company has saved its customers over 20 billion HKD in fuel costs.

data.title: Binance Launches UP Trading Competition with $200K in Rewards
data.description:
Binance Wallet announced on X the launch of its UP Trading Competition, offering participants the chance to win rewards totaling $200,000. The competition invites traders to engage in buying activities, with only buy volume being considered for the reward calculation. Participants can track their trade volume by clicking 'Join' on the Binance App event page.

The competition is designed to encourage active trading among users, focusing solely on buy transactions. This approach aims to stimulate market activity and provide traders with an opportunity to earn substantial rewards. The exclusion of sell volume from the competition criteria underscores the emphasis on purchasing activity, aligning with Binance's strategy to enhance trading engagement.

Participants are urged to take advantage of this opportunity, as the rewards are significant and the competition is expected to attract a large number of traders. The event is structured to ensure fair play and transparency, with the tracking mechanism available directly through the Binance App. This initiative reflects Binance's ongoing commitment to fostering a dynamic trading environment and rewarding user participation.

data.title: Hunan prosecutors have cracked a money laundering case involving over 6.84 million yuan, which used transactions involving Moutai liquor as a cover for transferring funds through virtual currencies.
data.description: A money laundering case involving virtual currency, prosecuted by the Yueyang District People's Procuratorate of Xiangtan City, Hunan Province, has been adjudicated in the first instance. The case involved over 6.84 million yuan, and all eight defendants were sentenced. The case revealed that the gang concealed the flow of funds by fabricating transactions involving Moutai liquor, converting proceeds from telecommunications fraud into virtual currency to return to upstream suppliers, and charging a commission of approximately 8% per transaction. The individuals involved had a division of labor, including fund transfer, providing tools, and disguising transactions. The court sentenced seven defendants to prison terms ranging from two to six years for concealing and covering up the proceeds of crime, while one defendant received a suspended sentence. (Huasheng Online)

data.title: Tata Motors to Increase Passenger Vehicle Prices from April 1
data.description: Tata Motors has announced a price increase for its passenger vehicles starting April 1. According to Jin10, the weighted average price of its fuel-powered vehicle lineup will rise by 0.5%. The decision comes as the company adjusts to various market factors and cost pressures. This move is expected to impact consumer purchasing decisions and the overall automotive market in India.

data.title: Iranian Gas Field Attack Prompts De-escalation Efforts
data.description: An attack on a significant Iranian gas field has potentially led to efforts to reduce the conflict's destructive impact. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that the incident may have influenced all parties involved to reconsider their strategies and seek ways to mitigate further escalation. The attack underscores the vulnerability of critical infrastructure in conflict zones and highlights the broader implications for regional stability and energy security. Observers are closely monitoring the situation to assess its impact on geopolitical dynamics and the global energy market.

data.title: New Opera by Gustavo Dudamel Explores Economic Themes
data.description: A new opera conducted by renowned conductor Gustavo Dudamel is set to captivate audiences with its unique focus on economic themes. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the innovative approach of blending classical music with economic narratives. The opera aims to offer a fresh perspective on economics, engaging audiences through a creative and artistic lens. Dudamel, known for his dynamic conducting style, brings a new dimension to the opera, promising an enriching experience for both music enthusiasts and those interested in economic discourse. The production is expected to draw attention for its novel concept and Dudamel's celebrated expertise in the field of music.

data.title: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright: Strategic Petroleum Reserve to Be Gradually Released in Coming Months
data.description: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright announced plans to gradually release the Strategic Petroleum Reserve over the next few months. According to Jin10, this decision aims to stabilize the energy market and address potential supply disruptions. The move comes amid ongoing discussions about energy security and market dynamics. The release strategy will be carefully managed to ensure minimal impact on global oil prices while supporting domestic energy needs. This approach reflects the administration's commitment to balancing energy supply and demand in a volatile market environment.

data.title: Impact of Trump Administration's Student Visa Pause on Business Schools
data.description: The Trump administration's decision to pause student visas is significantly affecting top business schools. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges these institutions face due to the policy. The pause has led to a decline in international student enrollment, impacting the diversity and financial stability of these schools. Many business schools are now grappling with the long-term implications of reduced international student presence, which traditionally contributes to a rich cultural exchange and financial resources. The policy has also prompted schools to explore alternative strategies to attract and retain international talent. As the situation unfolds, the broader educational landscape continues to adapt to these changes.

data.title: Banks Initiate $4.7 Billion Loan Sale for Sealed Air Acquisition
data.description: Banks have commenced the sale of a leveraged loan valued at nearly $4.7 billion to support the acquisition of packaging company Sealed Air. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial maneuver aimed at facilitating the transaction. The loan is part of a broader strategy to finance the acquisition, which underscores the ongoing activity in the mergers and acquisitions sector. The move reflects the banks' confidence in the deal's potential and the packaging industry's growth prospects. This development is a significant step in the acquisition process, marking a crucial phase in securing the necessary funding.

data.title: Economic Uncertainty Raises Concerns Over Job Security in the U.S.
data.description: Amid ongoing economic uncertainty, many Americans are increasingly concerned about their job security and the potential impact on their ability to save money or retire. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting these concerns as a significant issue for the U.S. workforce. In a recent episode of the Everybody’s Business podcast, Martha Gimbel discussed with hosts Svanek Smith and Chafkin her reasons for continuing to prefer living in the United States despite these challenges. The conversation underscores the broader anxiety felt by many regarding financial stability and future planning in the current economic climate.

data.title: UK March CBI Industrial Orders Show Slight Improvement
data.description: The UK's March CBI industrial orders balance recorded a value of -27, slightly better than the expected -30 and the previous -28. According to Jin10, this data indicates a marginal improvement in industrial orders compared to forecasts and the previous month. The CBI industrial orders balance is a key economic indicator that reflects the health of the manufacturing sector in the UK. Despite the improvement, the negative balance suggests ongoing challenges in the industry, as orders remain below zero, indicating contraction. Analysts will be closely monitoring these figures to assess the potential impact on the broader UK economy and manufacturing sector trends.

data.title: Bond Market Adjusts Expectations Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Bond investors are facing a shift in their expectations for monetary policy due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the situation is impacting financial markets. The anticipation of a more lenient monetary policy this year is being challenged as geopolitical tensions rise, affecting investor sentiment and market dynamics. The conflict has introduced uncertainty, prompting investors to reassess their strategies in light of potential economic implications. As the situation develops, market participants are closely monitoring the impact on global financial conditions.

data.title: Gemini Cuts 30% of Staff as Losses Widen to $585M in 2025
data.description: Gemini reduced its workforce by 30%, bringing total headcount down to roughly 445 employees since January. The company posted a $585M full-year loss while Q4 revenue beat analyst expectations at $60.3M, according to BeInCrypto.

data.title: Polymarket Sees $219,000 Bet Against U.S. Alien Confirmation by 2027
data.description: A newly established cryptocurrency address has placed a significant wager of approximately $219,000 on Polymarket, betting against the U.S. confirming the existence of aliens before 2027. According to NS3.AI, this address also made a smaller bet in a separate Polymarket market, wagering against the confirmation of alien existence by March 31st. These bets reflect skepticism about imminent official announcements regarding extraterrestrial life.

data.title: DeFiance Capital CEO Predicts Continued Pressure on Iran Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: DeFiance Capital CEO Arthur has expressed concerns over the ongoing tensions in the Middle East, suggesting that a short-term cooling is unlikely. According to NS3.AI, Arthur believes that the United States and Israel may continue to exert pressure on Iran, potentially leading to further disruptions in global supply chains in the coming weeks.

data.title: After approximately 14 years of inactivity, a whale transferred out 0.00079 BTC, leaving it with a floating profit of over $148 million on its holdings of approximately 2,100 BTC.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, address 1NB3ZX, after approximately 14 years of inactivity, made a transaction, transferring out 0.00079 BTC, worth about $56. This address received 2100 BTC on July 5, 2012, at a price of approximately $6.59. Its current holdings are worth approximately $148 million, representing a return of approximately 10,710 times.

data.title: Taiwan to Miss WTO Conference Over Delegation Labeling Dispute
data.description: Taiwan announced it will not attend a World Trade Organization (WTO) conference in Cameroon due to a disagreement over the labeling of its delegation in visa documents. Bloomberg posted on X that the issue arose when Cameroon's hosts referred to Taiwan's delegation in a manner that Taiwan found unacceptable. This decision underscores ongoing tensions related to Taiwan's international representation and the sensitivity surrounding its diplomatic status. Taiwan's absence from the conference highlights the challenges it faces in participating in global forums where its status is contested.

data.title: BlackRock deposited approximately 47,700 ETH and 544 BTC into Coinbase Prime.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock addresses deposited 47,728 ETH, worth approximately $102 million, and 544 BTC, worth approximately $38.3 million, into Coinbase Prime.
